source.definition: A building or enclosed space where vehicles are parked and protected from weather or theft.

explanation.sourceText: The English word "garage" comes from the French word "garage," which derives from the verb "garer," meaning "to park" or "to shelter."
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „garage” provine din francezul „garage”, care derivă de la verbul „garer”, însemnând „a parca” sau „a adăposti”.

target.definition: O clădire sau o încăpere în care se parchează și se adăpostesc vehicule.

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian word "garaj" was borrowed from the French "garage," which comes from the verb "garer" ("to park," "to shelter").
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul românesc „garaj” este împrumutat din francezul „garage”, care provine de la verbul „garer”, cu sensul de „a parca” sau „a adăposti”.

examples.sourceText: The garage is big enough for two cars.
examples.targetText: Garajul este suficient de mare pentru două mașini.

examples.sourceText: I parked my bike in the garage.
The garage has an electric gate.
We are building a new garage next to the house.
examples.targetText: Construim un garaj nou lângă casă.

learningTips.text: The Romanian word "garaj" sounds very similar to the English "garage"—imagine parking your car in a stylish French-inspired garage in Romania.
Remember that "garaj" in Romanian is pronounced with a soft 'j' sound at the end, like the 's' in 'massage'.
Think of your car getting a special "garaj" spot in Romania—this word is your key to safe parking.
Connect the Romanian "garaj" with the French verb "garer" meaning "to park"—this link makes the meaning easy to recall.
